Indicators: Organizational details
topic: The organization and its reporting practices
disclosure_title: Organizational details
value_quantitative: Land bank ~14,800 acres; net lettable area ~7.7 million sq. ft.; total sales RM3.3 billion; total assets RM15.9 billion; market capitalisation RM4.3 billion (as at 31 Dec 2023).
value_qualitative: Sime Darby Property Berhad (Malaysia) is a property developer evolving into a comprehensive real estate group, with operations across residential, commercial, industrial/logistics and leisure segments.

Indicators: Entities included in the organization’s sustainability reporting
topic: The organization and its reporting practices
disclosure_title: Entities included in the organization’s sustainability reporting
value_qualitative: Reporting boundary is based on ownership/management control and includes Corporate Operations and Direct Investment Portfolio under operational control; non-operational control assets are excluded, as are joint ventures/associates and businesses without >51% ownership/operational control.

Indicators: Reporting period, frequency and contact point
topic: The organization and its reporting practices
disclosure_title: Reporting period, frequency and contact point
value_qualitative: Report covers Sime Darby Property’s sustainability performance for FY2023; approved by the Board Sustainability Committee on 27 March 2024. Enquiries: sustainability@simedarbyproperty.com; website: www.simedarbyproperty.com.

Indicators: Restatements of information
topic: The organization and its reporting practices
disclosure_title: Restatements of information
value_quantitative: FY2022 (Restated) operational carbon emissions: 17,036 tCO2e (Scope 1: 754; Scope 2: 16,282).
value_qualitative: Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions for 2021 and 2022 were comprehensively reassessed following revised 2022 emissions factors announced in Dec 2023, and reporting was updated accordingly.

Indicators: External assurance
topic: The organization and its reporting practices
disclosure_title: External assurance
value_qualitative: The Sustainability Report was subjected to internal review by the Group Corporate Assurance Department and independent limited assurance by PricewaterhouseCoopers PLT (ISAE 3000 Revised) for selected indicators; see independent limited assurance report.

Indicators: Activities, value chain, and other business relationships
disclosure_title: Activities, value chain, and other business relationships
value_quantitative: 25 townships/developments nationwide; land bank ~14,800 acres; IAM manages ~7.7 million sq. ft. net lettable area.
value_qualitative: Key activities include property development, investment & asset management and leisure (e.g., KLGCC). Business relationships include strategic partnerships in industrial/logistics and participation in a Malaysian consortium to redevelop Battersea Power Station in London.

Indicators: Employees
disclosure_title: Employees
value_quantitative: Employee gender by category (2023): Senior Management female 36%; Middle Management female 46%; Executive female 54%; Non-Executive female 36%.
value_qualitative: Employee information is reported by category, including gender and age-group distributions in the Performance Data Table.

Indicators: Workers who are not employees
disclosure_title: Workers who are not employees
value_quantitative: 13 leadership visits interacted with 62 foreign workers (planned and surprise visits).
value_qualitative: The report describes contractor/foreign workers as part of HSSE programmes and commits to protecting contractor worker health (e.g., vaccination and worker quarters hygiene standards).

Indicators: Governance structure and composition
disclosure_title: Governance structure and composition
value_qualitative: Sustainability governance structure includes Board of Directors, Board Sustainability Committee, Management Committee and Management Sustainability Committee, supported by the Sustainability Unit, ESG champions and functional/business units.

Indicators: Nomination and selection of the highest governance body
disclosure_title: Nomination and selection of the highest governance body

Indicators: Chair of the highest governance body
disclosure_title: Chair of the highest governance body

Indicators: Role of the highest governance body in overseeing the management of impacts
disclosure_title: Role of the highest governance body in overseeing the management of impacts
value_qualitative: Board Sustainability Committee steers sustainability initiatives and embeds sustainability into decision-making and risk management, with quarterly ESG performance updates; ESG KPIs are tied to C-suite remuneration.

Indicators: Delegation of responsibility for managing impacts
disclosure_title: Delegation of responsibility for managing impacts
value_qualitative: Responsibilities for managing impacts are delegated through the Sustainability Department (ESG strategy, guidelines, monitoring via PMO), ESG Champions embedded in segments/functions, and the Management Sustainability Committee implementing ESG initiatives.

Indicators: Role of the highest governance body in sustainability reporting
disclosure_title: Role of the highest governance body in sustainability reporting
value_qualitative: The report states that FY2023 sustainability performance reporting was approved by the Board Sustainability Committee on 27 March 2024, indicating governance-level involvement in sustainability reporting.

Indicators: Conflicts of interest
disclosure_title: Conflicts of interest
value_qualitative: A Conflict of Interest Policy is described as ensuring fair and transparent decision-making by managing potential conflicts and preventing conflicts of interest from damaging the Group’s interests and reputation.

Indicators: Communication of critical concerns
disclosure_title: Communication of critical concerns
value_quantitative: 2023: 18 misconduct/malpractice reports received; 13 resolved (72%); 18 investigations conducted.
value_qualitative: Whistleblowing channels and investigations are managed by Group Corporate Assurance and reported directly to the Audit Committee; critical concerns/misconduct reporting outcomes are disclosed.

Indicators: Collective knowledge of the highest governance body
disclosure_title: Collective knowledge of the highest governance body
value_qualitative: Board members and senior management participated in training on leadership, global economic trends, sustainability, financial integrity, compliance standards, cybersecurity, innovation/AI, climate change and environmental awareness.

Indicators: Evaluation of the performance of the highest governance body
disclosure_title: Evaluation of the performance of the highest governance body

Indicators: Remuneration policies
disclosure_title: Remuneration policies
value_qualitative: Executive remuneration includes ESG Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) tied to remuneration of C-suite executives, emphasising emissions reduction and sustainable practices.

Indicators: Process to determine remuneration
disclosure_title: Process to determine remuneration

Indicators: Annual total compensation ratio
disclosure_title: Annual total compensation ratio

Indicators: Statement on sustainable development strategy
disclosure_title: Statement on sustainable development strategy
value_qualitative: Sustainability strategy is anchored on the Group’s purpose (“value multiplier”) and vision to advance real estate as a force for collective progress, with commitments including a 1.5°C-aligned transition that respects and protects nature and creates positive community impact.

Indicators: Policy commitments
disclosure_title: Policy commitments
value_qualitative: The Group sets policy commitments through a suite of sustainability policies/guidelines across material matters, including Sustainability Policy, Carbon Footprint Guidelines, Tree-to-Tree Replacement Policy, Waste & Water Management Guidelines, Human Rights Policy, Code of Business Conduct and Anti-Bribery & Corruption Policy.

Indicators: Embedding policy commitments
disclosure_title: Embedding policy commitments
value_qualitative: Policies and guidelines are implemented to ensure consistent integration and enforcement of sustainable practices across operations, aligned to the Group’s material matters and value chain activities.

Indicators: Processes to remediate negative impacts
disclosure_title: Processes to remediate negative impacts
value_qualitative: For data privacy and cybersecurity, the Group establishes processes to report security breaches, handle complaints and conduct thorough investigations to enable timely resolutions of security concerns/incidents.

Indicators: Mechanisms for seeking advice and raising concerns
disclosure_title: Mechanisms for seeking advice and raising concerns
value_qualitative: A dedicated whistleblowing channel is maintained for raising concerns and seeking advice; it is managed by Group Corporate Assurance under Audit Committee oversight, including investigation outcomes and Board updates.

Indicators: Compliance with laws and regulations
disclosure_title: Compliance with laws and regulations
value_qualitative: Compliance expectations are embedded in procurement: tender documents and Letters of Award require vendors to comply with relevant local authorities’ requirements, national laws and regulations (including social and environmental aspects) as part of vendor assessment.

Indicators: Membership associations
disclosure_title: Membership associations
value_qualitative: The Group participates in industry and business associations/platforms to advance sustainability dialogue and advocacy, including British Malaysia Chamber of Commerce (BMCC), CEO Action Network (CAN) and REHDA Institute engagements in 2023.

Indicators: Approach to stakeholder engagement
disclosure_title: Approach to stakeholder engagement
value_qualitative: The Group engages key stakeholders (employees, customers, investors, partners, regulators and communities) through tailored engagement strategies and multiple platforms with defined frequencies (e.g., ongoing, weekly, quarterly and annually) to incorporate feedback into operations and sustainability goals.

Indicators: Collective bargaining agreements
disclosure_title: Collective bargaining agreements
value_qualitative: Collective bargaining rights are upheld; the National Union of Commercial Workers (NUCW) represents a significant portion of the workforce, and the Group fosters constructive relationships with unions to support performance and operational efficiency.

Indicators: Process to determine material topics
disclosure_title: Process to determine material topics
value_qualitative: A double materiality assessment was conducted in 2023, assessing outside-in financial risks/opportunities and inside-out impacts; stakeholders were mapped and engaged with support from an independent consultant, and results were validated with Management and the Board.

Indicators: List of material topics
disclosure_title: List of material topics
value_qualitative: Assessment identified 13 topics; nine key material matters: climate adaptation; water management; OHS; diversity & inclusion; data privacy & cybersecurity; energy & carbon management; circularity; responsible supply chain; labour standards & practices.

Indicators: Management of material topics
disclosure_title: Management of material topics
value_qualitative: Management of material topics is addressed through the report’s performance sections across four areas of impact/value creation (economic, environment, social and governance), with specific topic sections describing commitments, approaches and progress for each material matter.

Indicators: Direct economic value generated and distributed
disclosure_title: Direct economic value generated and distributed

Indicators: Financial implications and other risks and opportunities due to climate change
disclosure_title: Financial implications and other risks and opportunities due to climate change
value_qualitative: The Group commenced evaluating the potential financial implications of climate change across selected assets, considering potential costs and opportunities, and assessing physical and transition risks using scenario analysis; climate risk is being integrated into the Enterprise Risk Management framework.

Indicators: Defined benefit plan obligations and other retirement plans
disclosure_title: Defined benefit plan obligations and other retirement plans

Indicators: Financial assistance received from government
disclosure_title: Financial assistance received from government

Indicators: Ratios of standard entry level wage by gender compared to local minimum wage
disclosure_title: Ratios of standard entry level wage by gender compared to local minimum wage

Indicators: Proportion of senior management hired from the local community
disclosure_title: Proportion of senior management hired from the local community

Indicators: Infrastructure investments and services supported
disclosure_title: Infrastructure investments and services supported
value_quantitative: 2023: RM9,482,170 invested into community initiatives; 68,377 beneficiaries; 778 employee volunteers; 2,941 volunteer hours.
value_qualitative: Community investments and programmes are used to support community well-being and engagement (e.g., educational support, environmental initiatives and social programmes) as part of the Community Experience material matter.

Indicators: Significant indirect economic impacts
disclosure_title: Significant indirect economic impacts

Indicators: Proportion of spending on local suppliers
disclosure_title: Proportion of spending on local suppliers
value_quantitative: 2023: Total procurement spend RM2,176.96 million; total payment to local vendors RM2,180.70 million; proportion local suppliers 99.83%.
value_qualitative: Local vendors are vendors whose registered businesses are incorporated in Malaysia.

Indicators: Operations assessed for risks related to corruption
disclosure_title: Operations assessed for risks related to corruption
value_quantitative: 2023: 20 operations assessed for corruption risks (100%*).

Indicators: Communication and training about anti-corruption policies and procedures
disclosure_title: Communication and training about anti-corruption policies and procedures
value_quantitative: 2023: 1,284 employees received mandatory anti-corruption training (84.5%).
value_qualitative: Mandatory ABC training is required for all employees and reinforced via annual COBC e-learning/e-assessment and targeted communications (e.g., No Gift Policy during festive seasons).

Indicators: Confirmed incidents of corruption and actions taken
disclosure_title: Confirmed incidents of corruption and actions taken
value_quantitative: 2023: 0 confirmed incidents of corruption; 0 employees dismissed/disciplined for corruption; 0 fines/penalties/settlements related to corruption.
value_qualitative: The Group maintains a commitment to transparency and accountability in anti-corruption reporting.

Indicators: Legal actions for anti-competitive behavior, anti-trust, and monopoly practices
disclosure_title: Legal actions for anti-competitive behavior, anti-trust, and monopoly practices

Indicators: Approach to tax
disclosure_title: Approach to tax

Indicators: Tax governance, control, and risk management
disclosure_title: Tax governance, control, and risk management

Indicators: Stakeholder engagement and management of concerns related to tax
disclosure_title: Stakeholder engagement and management of concerns related to tax

Indicators: Country-by-country reporting
disclosure_title: Country-by-country reporting

Indicators: Materials used by weight or volume
disclosure_title: Materials used by weight or volume

Indicators: Recycled input materials used
disclosure_title: Recycled input materials used
value_qualitative: Construction sites establish sorting stations for segregation and recycling of materials (e.g., concrete, metal, wood) and recycle materials from renovations for new constructions and landscaping projects.

Indicators: Reclaimed products and their packaging materials
disclosure_title: Reclaimed products and their packaging materials

Indicators: Energy consumption within the organization
disclosure_title: Energy consumption within the organization

Indicators: Energy consumption outside of the organization
disclosure_title: Energy consumption outside of the organization

Indicators: Energy intensity
disclosure_title: Energy intensity

Indicators: Reduction of energy consumption
disclosure_title: Reduction of energy consumption
value_quantitative: Estimated energy reductions: 3,500 kWh annually per location (LED streetlights); 80,000 kWh reduction over 3 months (T8 bulbs at Leisure Complex); 3,500 kWh annual reduction via light sensors (or 1,750 kWh for half-year).
value_qualitative: Energy efficiency retrofits include lighting upgrades, energy audits and planned rooftop solar PV installations across identified assets.

Indicators: Reduction in energy requirements of products and services
disclosure_title: Reduction in energy requirements of products and services

Indicators: Interactions with water as a shared resource
disclosure_title: Interactions with water as a shared resource

Indicators: Management of water discharge-related impacts
disclosure_title: Management of water discharge-related impacts
value_qualitative: Water quality is monitored under Department of Environment Environmental Management Plans, and wastewater/effluent discharge is ensured to meet regulatory requirements and EIA Approval Conditions.

Indicators: Water withdrawal
disclosure_title: Water withdrawal
value_quantitative: Potable water withdrawal (ML): 2022 1,205.80; 2023 986.00; 18.23% reduction (potable).
value_qualitative: Potable withdrawal decreased due to increased use of non-potable sources (e.g., rainwater harvesting); non-potable measurement system is under development and not disclosed this year.

Indicators: Operational sites owned, leased, managed in, or adjacent to, protected areas and areas of high biodiversity value outside protected areas
disclosure_title: Operational sites owned, leased, managed in, or adjacent to, protected areas and areas of high biodiversity value outside protected areas
value_quantitative: Areas allocated for urban biodiversity focus (Table 2): TOTAL 941.90 ac; 3,811,737.43 m2; ecological corridors length 18.45 km.
value_qualitative: Appendix table lists biodiversity-focused operational sites/areas of interest (parks, wetlands, corridors and green belts) by township and business unit.

Indicators: Significant impacts of activities, products, and services on biodiversity
disclosure_title: Significant impacts of activities, products, and services on biodiversity
value_quantitative: 2023 impact: 942 acres restored/conserved; 4 ecological corridors identified (18.45 km); over 140,000 trees planted across townships.
value_qualitative: Report describes biodiversity initiatives and outcomes across developments, including corridor enhancement and community engagement via citizen scientist projects.

Indicators: Habitats protected or restored
disclosure_title: Habitats protected or restored
value_quantitative: Urban biodiversity areas protected/restored: TOTAL 941.90 acres (Table 2); includes KL East Park 53 acres and multiple parks/wetlands.
value_qualitative: Habitats are created/enhanced through conservation and regeneration initiatives across townships (e.g., parks, wetlands, ecological corridors).

Indicators: IUCN Red List species and national conservation list species with habitats in areas affected by operations
disclosure_title: IUCN Red List species and national conservation list species with habitats in areas affected by operations
value_quantitative: 17 species with conservation concerns (IUCN Red List); threatened trees planted increased from 19,520 (2019) to 26,085 (2023); 53 species identified and planted.
value_qualitative: Report references the IUCN Red List in describing conservation concern species and planting of threatened tree species as part of ex-situ efforts.

Indicators: Direct (Scope 1) GHG emissions
disclosure_title: Direct (Scope 1) GHG emissions
value_quantitative: Scope 1 emissions (tCO2e): FY2022 (restated) 754; FY2023 537 (table shows 537.15).

Indicators: Energy indirect (Scope 2) GHG emissions
disclosure_title: Energy indirect (Scope 2) GHG emissions
value_quantitative: Scope 2 emissions (tCO2e): FY2022 (restated) 16,282; FY2023 15,850 (table shows 15,849.50).

Indicators: Other indirect (Scope 3) GHG emissions
disclosure_title: Other indirect (Scope 3) GHG emissions
value_qualitative: To inform its Net Zero strategy, the Group is assessing key Scope 3 emissions, starting with waste diverted from landfill and employee commuting, with plans to expand analysis through collaboration with tenants, contractors and suppliers.

Indicators: Waste generation and significant waste-related impacts
disclosure_title: Waste generation and significant waste-related impacts
value_quantitative: Waste 2023: 14,402.01 t; landfilling 75.14%; diverted from landfill 24.86%; key types: operational solid waste 49%, food waste 16%.
value_qualitative: Circularity section explains waste handling methods and waste stream composition, highlighting landfill dominance and ongoing recycling/composting initiatives.

Indicators: Management of significant waste related impacts
disclosure_title: Management of significant waste related impacts
value_qualitative: Waste impacts are managed via contractor collaboration to reuse/recycle construction waste, separation at source during site clearance, 3R practices for organic landscaping waste, resident campaigns (including no single-use plastic) and improved waste data traceability.

Indicators: Waste generated
disclosure_title: Waste generated
value_quantitative: Total waste generated in 2023: 14,402.01 tonnes.

Indicators: Waste diverted from disposal
disclosure_title: Waste diverted from disposal
value_quantitative: Waste reused and recycled in 2023: 3,580.15 tonnes; 24.86% diverted from landfill via recycling initiatives including composting.

Indicators: Waste directed to disposal
disclosure_title: Waste directed to disposal
value_quantitative: Waste directed to disposal in 2023: 10,821.86 tonnes; landfilling accounts for 75.14% of waste stream.

Indicators: New employee hires and employee turnover
disclosure_title: New employee hires and employee turnover
value_quantitative: 2023 new hires by position level: Senior Mgmt 26; Middle Mgmt 42; Executive 77; Non-Executive 70. 2023 turnover by position level: Senior Mgmt 25; Middle Mgmt 40; Executive 47; Non-Executive 41.

Indicators: Parental leave
disclosure_title: Parental leave
value_quantitative: Return-to-work rates after parental leave (%): 2021 98; 2022 100; 2023 100. Retention rates 12 months after return (%): 2021 83; 2022 100; 2023 98.

Indicators: Occupational health and safety management system
disclosure_title: Occupational health and safety management system
value_qualitative: Occupational health and safety is managed under a QHSSE policy and unified department; an HSSE Manual Management System integrates HSSE standards across operations and supply chain, supported by safety KPIs in scorecards and performance evaluations.

Indicators: Hazard identification, risk assessment, and incident investigation
disclosure_title: Hazard identification, risk assessment, and incident investigation
value_qualitative: The report cites ongoing risk assessments and monitoring to refine HSSE practices. Safety Observations require scanning the environment for hazards, documenting safe/unsafe conditions and capturing photos of identified risks.

Indicators: Worker participation, consultation, and communication on occupational health and safety
disclosure_title: Worker participation, consultation, and communication on occupational health and safety
value_quantitative: 2023: 1,765 toolbox talk sessions; attendance 132,283 (incl. Taiso); Safety Day participants >750; Partners’ Dialogue Day attendees 115.
value_qualitative: Workers and contractors are engaged on safety through regular toolbox talks, pre-work exercises and safety events, alongside leadership site engagement to reinforce HSSE culture.

Indicators: Worker training on occupational health and safety
disclosure_title: Worker training on occupational health and safety
value_quantitative: Safety training: 3,437 man-hours provided; 412 employees trained on health & safety standards (FY2023).

Indicators: Work-related injuries
disclosure_title: Work-related injuries
value_quantitative: 2023: Fatality 1; Lost Time Injuries 11; Total injuries and first aid reported 194; LTIFR increased to 0.57 (from 0.11 in 2021).
value_qualitative: Reported injuries increased alongside improved contractor engagement and more transparent incident reporting; the report notes an excavation collapse fatality in Dec 2023.

Indicators: Work-related ill health
disclosure_title: Work-related ill health

disclosure_title: Average hours of training per year per employe

disclosure_title: Diversity of governance bodies and employees

Indicators: Substantiated complaints concerning breaches of customer privacy and losses of customer data
disclosure_title: Substantiated complaints concerning breaches of customer privacy and losses of customer data
value_quantitative: Number of substantiated complaints concerning breaches of customer privacy and losses of customer data (2023): 1.
value_qualitative: In 2023, the Group investigated two data privacy complaints through internal processes and engaged complainants; investigations found no evidence to support the claims.
